pattern: Backtracking grows a partition by cutting off every leading palindromic prefix and recursing on the rest, yielding all palindrome partitions of the string.
hints: At each position decide where the first piece ends; it is only allowed if that prefix is a palindrome, then solve the remaining suffix the same way.
Recurse from a start index: for every end such that s[start..end] is a palindrome, append it and recurse from end+1, backtracking afterwards.
Use a depth-first search carrying the current list of pieces; when start reaches the end of the string record a copy, otherwise try each palindromic prefix and recurse.

edge_inputs.description: two partitions, one all-single and one with a pair
single character has exactly one partition
all distinct characters allow only the singleton partition
whole string is a palindrome, many partitions
repeated pairs maximise branching for the size

stress.description: short two-letter strings keep the exponential partition count bounded
stress.note: length capped at 12 over a two-letter alphabet so the full partition set stays small enough to enumerate
